2605.29076.title: Structured Prompt Optimization Meets Reinforcement Learning for Global and Local Interpretability over Complex Text
2605.29076.paper_summary: LLMs have advanced text classification, yet existing paradigms face a trade-off: supervised (label only) fine-tuning is scalable but offers limited reasoning on complex text and lacks broader model transparency, while discrete prompt optimization offers human-readable instructions but struggles with performance and scalability. We introduce eXTC (eXplainable Text Classifier) with three progressive stages: (1) learning a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP, or rulebook) in natural language via a new Structured Prompt Optimization algorithm; (2) SOP-grounded reasoning distillation from a large teacher LLM into a compact LM; and (3) expanding reasoning capabilities beyond the initial SOP via reinforcement learning. This design enables eXTC to provide (i) fast inference via a compact LM, with (ii) inference-time local reasoning traces, alongside a global, modular explanation of its learned domain rules, while (iii) significantly outperforming existing paradigms across diverse benchmarks in both classification performance and explanation quality, with stage-by-stage gains.
2605.29076.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）已經推進了文本分類，但現有的範式面臨著一個權衡：監督式（僅標籤）微調具有可擴展性，但在複雜文本上的推理能力有限，且缺乏更廣泛的模型透明度，而離散提示優化則提供了人類可讀的指示，但在性能和可擴展性方面存在困難。我們介紹了eXTC（可解釋文本分類器），其具有三個漸進階段：（1）通過一種新的結構化提示優化算法，在自然語言中學習標準操作程序（SOP或規則手冊）；（2）從大型教師LLM中提取基於SOP的推理蒸餾到一個緊湊的LM；以及（3）通過強化學習擴展推理能力，超越初始SOP。這一設計使eXTC能夠提供（i）通過緊湊的LM實現快速推理，並具有（ii）推理時的局部推理痕跡，以及其學習的領域規則的全局模塊化解釋，同時（iii）在分類性能和解釋質量方面顯著超越現有範式，在各種基準測試中實現階段性增長。
2605.29076.author: Tianyang Zhou et.al.
2605.29076.authors: Tianyang Zhou, Wenbo Chen, Pierre Jinghong Liang, Leman Akoglu
